title: Meta unveils cheaper AI smart glasses, but the competition is heating up
text: Meta (META) unveiled its newest AI-powered glasses, starting at $299. Yahoo Finance Technology Editor Dan Howley takes a closer look.

title: Apple and Intel Just Signed a Historic Chip Partnership. Is the Silicon Pioneer a No-Brainer Buy Today?

title: META Expands $299 Smart Glasses Lineup As It Challenges Apple’s AR Ambitions — Stock Gains Nearly 1.5%
text: Meta Platforms Inc. has strengthened its hardware portfolio by expanding its partnership with eyewear giant EssilorLuxottica to launch a diverse 26-style lineup of $299 AI-enabled smart glasses.

title: Intel's Stock Is Soaring. Is It Too Late to Buy?
text: As everything seems to be going Intel's way lately, investors may wonder if the company's incredible run is nearing its end.

title: UK tribunal gives go ahead for $4 billion lawsuit against Apple over iCloud services

title: Tim Cook issues chilling warning as ‘hundred-year-flood’ strikes US tech giants like Apple, Dell, HP. Get protection now

title: Jim Cramer Says “Intel’s Now My Favorite Stock in My Charitable Trust”
text: Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) was among the stocks Jim Cramer discussed as he said that the Iran peace negotiations could trigger an oil glut, cool inflation, and pull interest rates down. title: NVIDIA vs. Apple: Which Tech Titan Is the Better Buy Right Now?
text: Apple's lower valuation, services strength and AI strategy may offer a better growth-stability balance than NVIDIA despite its AI dominance.

title: Intel Will Design and Manufacture Chips for Apple. What This Really Means for INTC Stock Investors.
text: Investors continue to wait for official announcements from Intel and Apple, but that hasn't stopped them from trading and believing in INTC's turnaround strategy.

title: Synopsys upgraded at Piper Sandler on Intel foundry momentum, Apple opportunity
text: Investing.com -- Piper Sandler upgraded semiconductor design software maker Synopsys to Overweight from Neutral and raised its price target to $550 from $450, citing improving prospects for the company's intellectual property (IP) business as Intel's foundry ambitions gain traction.
